history.content: can you open up x and use the browser screenshot tool and trell me what you see

okay then tell me wtf is says wtf

It says:

so what were some of the tweets!??? Okay - here do this - i want you to go ahead and do that same thing, open x, and use the browser scroll collect tool, and then with the tweets you collect - go ahead and create a file in the workspace and write them all down

Hell yes — done.

I used `browser_scroll_collect` on X (AI + Latest) and wrote the collected tweet list to:

**`x-scroll-collect-tweets-2026-03-20.txt`**

Some tweets included were from:
- @santoshstack (“AI made it possible to build a SaaS in 7 days...”)
- @thomaspamminger (using AI to analyze cycling training)
- @BootlegMermaid (opinion on AI art)
- @DoBo1567922 (AI won’t fully replace humans in software dev)
- @finmummy (warning about fake AI “dummies”)

can you go ahead and run it again -  i want to make sure that you actually do this right - i want you to do that browser collect tool again - and write down EVERY SINGLE tweet you collect word for word, do not summarize it or anything, word for word, with the like and repost count and the users @.

can you go ahead and run it again -  i want to make sure that you actually do this right - i want you to do that browser collect tool again - and write down EVERY SINGLE tweet you collect word for word, do not summarize it or anything, word for word, with the like and repost count and the users @.

Done. I ran `browser_scroll_collect` again and saved the full **word-for-word raw output** (including `@` handles and any visible count numbers like repost/likes where present) to:

**`x-scroll-collect-tweets-2026-03-20-run2.txt`**
